name: Principality of Roaz

street_level_faction_presence.street_signals: Symbols, uniform cues, work marks, and rumor shorthand make power visible in daily movement.

greetings: Direct acknowledgment and role-first introductions are favored over ornament.
hospitality_rules: Hospitality is structured, documented, and often conditional on lawful status or known purpose.
insult_triggers: wasting state labor or strategic resources
taboo_topics: casual discussion of covert enforcement methods
class_expectations: officials are expected to be composed and exact
workers are expected to obey lane, schedule, and checkpoint rules
faith_etiquette: quiet respect in civic-religious spaces; spectacle is less valued than discipline
outsider_mistakes: speaking too freely near enforcement halls

occupation_and_daily_life.daily_rhythm: Daily life is shaped by shifts, inspections, filings, quarry traffic, foundry schedules, and legal process.
occupation_and_daily_life.work_rhythm_by_calendar_or_season: Winter constrains extraction and movement while temperate seasons accelerate transport, construction, and adjudication.
occupation_and_daily_life.ordinary_texture: Air carries coal, oil, hot iron, damp paper, and ration kitchens. Streets sound like boots, carts, hammers, whistles, and ordered commands.

travel_and_transition_texture: Road entry into Roaz feels increasingly measured: more checkpoints, stronger document culture, and visible consequence for evasion.
dwelling_and_settlement_life: Homes are plainer and more compartmentalized by class, with secure storage, work tools, and practical lighting prioritized over display.
relationship_and_reputation_logic: People are remembered for reliability, lawful precision, and whether they create risk for their associates.
region_specific_emotional_tone: Roaz stories naturally emerge as pressure-cooker dramas of law, secrecy, state power, suspicion, civic duty, and difficult restraint.
